skill.name: lipsync
skill.description: Lip-sync a face to a specific audio track on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes across ByteDance OmniHuman (audio-driven full-body avatar from a portrait + audio), Sync Labs sync v2 / Pro (state-of-the-art mouth sync onto a video), Kling lipsync (audio-to- video and text-to-video with synced speech), and Creatify lipsync. The skill picks the right endpoint for the user's actual intent — portrait still + audio (avatar-style), source video + audio (mouth- swap on existing footage), or generate-and-sync from a script. Triggers on "lip sync", "lipsync", "make this video speak", "match audio to mouth", "dub video", "sync lips to voice", "Sync Labs", "voiceover sync", or any explicit ask to drive a face's mouth from an audio track.
skill.summary: Synchronize any face to an audio track using the RunComfy CLI, routing across OmniHuman, Sync Labs, Kling, and Creatify models based on user intent.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 97 static findings are false positives. External command hits (51) are `runcomfy run` invocations in documentation code blocks, constrained by allowed-tools Bash(runcomfy *). Network hits (32) are URLs to legitimate RunComfy infrastructure. Filesystem hits (2) reference ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json in the security documentation section. Weak crypto and system reconnaissance hits are markdown text, not code. The skill responsibly documents dual-use consent and security practices.

low_findings.title: Shell command execution in documentation
low_findings.description: The skill uses `runcomfy run` commands in markdown code blocks for documentation purposes. These are legitimate usage examples constrained by the allowed-tools declaration. No command injection vector exists as inputs pass through JSON via --input flag.

low_findings.title: Network requests to external API
low_findings.description: The skill references URLs on runcomfy.com and skills.sh domains. These are legitimate endpoints for the RunComfy platform. The security section explicitly documents that outbound endpoints are restricted to *.runcomfy.net and *.runcomfy.com only.

low_findings.title: Filesystem token path reference
low_findings.description: Line 220 references ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json in the Security & Privacy section. This documents the token storage location for transparency. The skill does not read or write this file directly.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dual-use technology consent concern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides lip-sync capabilities that could be misused for creating synthetic media without consent. The skill author has addressed this with a consent section and usage guidelines, but the technology itself is dual-use.

content.user_title: Lip-sync any face to audio with AI video models
content.value_statement: Creating lip-synced videos typically requires navigating multiple different AI models with incompatible APIs. This skill picks the right RunComfy model for your input type and executes the correct runcomfy command, supporting portrait-to-avatar, video mouth-swap, and script-to-video workflows.
actual_capabilities: Select the optimal lip-sync model based on input type (portrait + audio, video + audio, or script only)
Execute runcomfy run commands for OmniHuman, Sync Labs v2/Pro, Kling, and Creatify models
Generate talking-head avatar videos from a single portrait photo and audio track
Replace mouth movements in existing video footage with new audio using Sync Labs
Produce synced video from a text script when no audio file is available
Support foreign-language dubbing workflows with translated voiceover and original video
limitations: Requires the runcomfy CLI to be installed and authenticated before use
Cannot process or validate the source video or audio files for quality
Does not generate video content from scratch for talking-head avatars beyond the OmniHuman pipeline
Sync quality depends heavily on source audio clarity and source audio/video duration matching

use_cases.title: Foreign-language video dubbing
use_cases.description: Take an existing brand video with translated voiceover audio and produce a lip-synced version where the speaker appears to speak the new language naturally.

use_cases.title: AI avatar content creation
use_cases.description: Create a talking-head video from a single portrait photo and a voiceover audio file for UGC ads, product demos, or virtual presenter content.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Script-to-video with synced speech
use_cases.description: Write a script and generate a complete video with synced speech audio, no separate audio file needed, using Kling text-to-video or HappyHorse models.
use_cases.target_user: Video producers working from written content

prompt_templates.title: Basic lip-sync with existing video and audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Lip sync this video with this audio file
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a source video and a voiceover audio track and wants the mouth to match the new audio

prompt_templates.title: Talking-head avatar from portrait
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking head video from this portrait photo and voiceover audio
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a single portrait image and wants an avatar that speaks the provided audio

prompt_templates.title: Multi-language dubbing workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Dub this brand video into Spanish using this translated voiceover file
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to replace mouth movements in an existing video to match a translated audio track in a different language

prompt_templates.title: Script-based generation with no audio file
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video where a person says this script and sync the lips to the spoken words
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a written script but no pre-recorded audio file and wants a complete video with synced speech

output_examples.input: A product manager has a 30-second brand video in English and a French voiceover track. They ask to dub the video.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Sync Labs sync v2 Pro (the default for video mouth-swap), constructs the runcomfy run command with video_url pointing to the source video and audio_url pointing to the French voiceover, and outputs a new video file where the speaker mouth matches the French audio while preserving all original camera movement, lighting, and background.

output_examples.input: A content creator has a headshot photo and a 15-second product pitch audio recording. They want to create an avatar-style video.
output_examples.output: The skill selects OmniHuman (the default for portrait + audio avatar-style), runs the command with image_url and audio_url inputs, and produces a talking-head video where the portrait naturally speaks and gestures to the audio track.

best_practices: Use clean voiceover audio without background music for the best lip-sync quality
Match audio track duration to video duration to prevent drift in mouth synchronization
Confirm the speaker in the audio has consented to having their voice paired with the target face before processing
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to lip-sync videos of real public figures without explicit consent for the specific use
Do not use a low-resolution or heavily compressed source video when high-quality output is required
Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell; install the runcomfy CLI only from the verified npm package

faq.question: What tools do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need the runcomfy CLI installed globally via npm and an active RunComfy account. Install with 'npm i -g @runcomfy/cli' and sign in with 'runcomfy login'.

faq.question: Which model will be used for my task?
faq.answer: The skill picks the model based on your input type. Portrait still plus audio uses OmniHuman. Source video plus audio uses Sync Labs v2 Pro. Script only uses Kling text-to-video or HappyHorse.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with any video file?
faq.answer: You can use any video file hosted at a URL. The skill does not upload files directly. You must provide a publicly accessible URL to your source video and audio files.

faq.question: What is the difference between Sync Labs v2 and v2 Pro?
faq.answer: Sync Labs v2 Pro is the premium tier with higher quality mouth motion tracking. Use v2 Pro for final delivery and v2 for scaled batch jobs or drafts where cost is a concern.

faq.question: Can I create a talking head without a video, just from a photo?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a portrait photo and an audio file. The skill routes to OmniHuman, which generates a full-body talking avatar from a single still image and audio track.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use for any content?
faq.answer: No. Lip-sync technology is dual-use. Only process content where the person appearing in the video has consented. Do not target real public figures without permission or create defamatory content.
